response: {"calibration_score":0.37,"overconfident_count":4,"underconfident_count":0,"well_calibrated_count":7,"biases_detected":["optimism_bias: assumed complex jq heredoc with nested quotes would work without prior syntax validation (bc59cf34-8, multiple Write/Bash retries before success)","availability_bias: used sleep-based polling pattern (sleep 60 && tail) despite Monitor/TaskOutput tools being available and sleep polling being explicitly discouraged in config (bc59cf34-24)","sunk_cost: continued iterating on broken jq aggregator script rather than pivoting to simpler Python approach that was already proven effective in the same session (bc59cf34-8)","anchoring: assumed relative-path bash commands would resolve correctly without verifying CWD first, causing a cluster of preventable ENOENT failures (bc59cf34-20, bc59cf34-27)"],"recommendations":["Validate shell escaping and heredoc syntax in isolated test before embedding in multi-step workflows — especially when mixing jq filters with bash variable interpolation","Replace sleep+tail polling with TaskOutput or Monitor tool calls; the sleep 60 command in bc59cf34-24 timed out and forced a pivot that could have been avoided","When 2+ consecutive bash commands fail with path/permission errors, switch to absolute-path Read/Glob/Grep tools immediately rather than retrying bash with modified flags","Before running npm test or any relative-path command via Bash, emit a pwd check or use absolute cd prefix — CWD drift between tool calls caused multiple avoidable failures across bc59cf34 session"]}
